options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: coop
options.description:
Enables cooping a seed by making local items the same as remote items, enabling
your friends to receive items you pick up.  Also helpful if trying to play a world from multiple devices.
You will still need to pickup djinn the other person picks up.
off - vanilla
progression - Only progression items are treated as remote items
prog_useful - Progression and useful items are treated as remote items
all - All items are treated as remote items

options.name: item_shuffle
options.description:
Determine which locations in the game are part of the pool.
All Chests and Tablets: includes all chests and tablets and does not include items from pots, barrels, dug up. Roughly 100 less locations to check from All Items
All Items: includes everything from the vanilla game, including items in pots, barrels, scooped up, covered in leaves or on the overworld. Roughly 320 locations in total.

options.name: omit_locations
options.description:
Choose to omit locations containing optional harder boss fights
No Omission: keeps all super bosses in play.
Omit Anemos Inner Sanctum: omits Anemos Inner Sanctum. Removes roughly 4 locations from the pool.
Omit Superbosses and Inner Sanctum: omits all super bosses and Anemos Inner Sanctum. Removes roughly 7 locations from the pool

options.name: add_elvenshirt_clericsring
options.display_name: Add Elven Shirt and Cleric's ring
options.description: Adds the Elven Shirt and Cleric's Ring from Golden Sun to the item pool.

options.name: add_non_obtainable_items
options.description:
Adds a variety of items that are normally unobtainable through normal means in the game.
These are: Casual Shirt, Golden Boots, Aroma Ring, Golden Shirt, Ninja Sandals, Golden Ring, 
Herbed Shirt, Knight's Greave, Rainbow Ring, Divine Camisole, Silver Greave and Soul Ring

options.name: goal
options.description:
What conditions must be satisfied for this world to goal.  You can select however many you want, and only
when all the selected conditions are satisfied is the world considered goaled.

Valid options are:
    - "Chestbeaters"
    - "King Scorpion"
    - "Briggs"
    - "Aqua Hydra"
    - "Poseidon"
    - "Serpent"
    - "Avimander"
    - "Moapa"
    - "Reunion"
    - "Flame Dragons"
    - "Doom Dragon"
    - "Star Magician"
    - "Sentinel"
    - "Valukar"
    - "Dullahan"
    - "Djinn Hunt"
    - "Summon Hunt"

IMPORTANT:
1. If Doom Dragon is a selected goal, make sure you have a save prior to the fight available if you don't
end the world on this fight.  We take no responsibility for borked experiences due to save mismanagement.
2. If any of the non-Dullahan superbosses are selected as a goal, then omit_locations is set to 1 (if not 0).
If Dullahan is a goal, then the omit_locations option is set to 0.

options.name: random_goals
options.description:
From the goals configured in "goals", pick the configured number of the goals randomly.
0 disables random picking, and all the configured goals are selected.

options.name: djinn_hunt_count
options.description: How many djinn you need to obtain in order to satisfy the Djinn Hunt Objective

options.name: summon_hunt_count
options.description: How many summons you need to obtain in order to satisfy the Summon Hunt Objective

options.name: reveal_hidden_item
options.description:
Require Reveal to be able to logically access hidden items.
Note that most hidden items can be gotten regardless of having Reveal or not.
Also there are a few locations that hard require Reveal to be obtainable, this setting does not alter those.

options.name: shortcut_mars_lighthouse
options.description:
The 4 elemental wing puzzles to unlock the teleport pad can be accessed with just the Mars Star instead of requiring to heatup the lighthouse.
Note the lighthouse is still frozen so the Mars Djinni Fugue is still inaccessible and requires heating up the lighthouse.

options.name: shortcut_magma_rock
options.description:
The pushable pillars at the groundfloor and 1st floor in Magma Rock are pushed downwards allowing you to access the Interior immediately without navigating the exterior.
Note that you can not leave Magma Rock interior from the top to get outside at the top of Magma Rock exterior.

options.name: lemurian_ship
options.description:
What needs to be done to get the ship?
Vanilla: requires getting the Black Crystal and completing the Gabomba Statue to get the reward from Madras Mayor to be able to enter the ship and activate it.
Ship Door Unlocked: The ship door is unlocked but you still require to activate the ship by reaching the engine room. Black Crystal is no longer in the pool as it is not required.
Available From The Start: allows you to use the ship from the beginning of the game. Black Crystal is no longer in the pool as it is not required.

options.name: start_with_wings_of_anemos
options.description: When enabled, the ship starts with the Wings of Anemos.

options.name: anemos_inner_sanctum_access
options.description:
Determine accesss to Anemos Inner Sanctun
Vanilla: requires all 72 Djinn to be able to enter Anemos Inner Sanctum
Randomized: will select a value between 16 to 28 Djinn to be able to access Anemos Inner Sanctum
Open: allows you to enter Anemos Inner Sanctum without any Djinn

options.name: djinn_logic
options.description:
How much do Djinn affect logic for being able to defeat bosses?
Assuming this is set to 100 (Normal) beating Briggs expects 6 djinn, Poseidon 24 djinn and Doom Dragon 56 djinn.
Dullahan goes up to 72 (All Djinn in the game). Setting this to 50 (Hard) will halve all of these numbers and 0 will remove the requirement completely.

options.name: name_puzzles
options.description:
Determines how the named puzzles will generate. These include the puzzles for Gambomba Statue, Gaia Rock and Trial Road
Vanilla: uses the name that is given to Felix
Fixed: will force the puzzles to use the name 'Felix'
Randomized: will force the puzzles to be a random name

options.name: shuffle_characters
options.description:
Where can you find the other characters?
Vanilla: makes it like the vanilla experience.
Vanilla Shuffled: puts the characters in each others locations.
Anywhere: puts the characters in the multiworld itempool,
note that Jenna's character location is forced to be a character due to game limiations with psynergy learning.

options.name: second_starting_character
options.description:
Which character will join Felix on Idejima?
This will always be Jenna when Character Shuffle is set to Vanilla, otherwise it will be whichever character this is set to.

options.name: scale_characters
options.description: Whether to scale character levels by spheres.  Increases generation time.

options.name: max_scaled_level
options.description: The maximum level a scaled character should have.  Only valid if scale_characters is true.

options.name: character_stats
options.description:
Determine the stats for characters
Vanilla: leaves the stats as per the vanilla game.
Shuffle Character Stats: will shuffle stats between characters.
Adjust Character Stats: will randomize stats for each character within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: character_elements
options.description:
Determine how character elements are shuffled
Vanilla: leaves character elements as per the vanilla game
Shuffle Character Elements: will shuffle them between characters
Randomize Character Elements: will randomize each characters element

options.name: randomize_class_stat_boosts
options.description: When enabled the base stats for classes are randomized

options.name: no_util_psynergy_from_classes
options.description: Prevents utility Psynergy (Growth, Frost, etc.) from being learned by classes.

options.name: class_psynergy
options.description:
Determine what Psynergy a class will learn
Vanilla: leaves the Psynergy on their vanilla classes
Randomize Psynergy by Classline: Learned Psynergy is shuffled by classlines (e.g. Squire will have Brute Psynergy)
Randomize by Psynergy Group: Learned Psynergy is shuffled by Psynergy groups (e.g. if a class learns Spire, it will also learn Clay Spire)
Randomize by Psynergy Group Prefer Element: As by group, but with a preference to the same Element
Randomize by Psynergy Element: Learned Psynergy is freely shuffled with another Psynergy of the same element
Fully Randomize it will randomize it completely without grouping or preference

choices.name: randomize_by_psynergy_group_prefer_element
choices.display_name: Randomize By Psynergy Group Prefer Element

options.name: psynergy_levels
options.description:
Determine when Psynergy is learned
Vanilla: leaves the learning of Psynergy on the vanilla levels.
Adjust Learning Levels: will randomize learning levels within a margin from vanilla.
Randomize Learning Levels: will randomize learning levels completely.

options.name: adjust_psynergy_power
options.description: When enabled the power of Psynergy is randomized within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: adjust_psynergy_cost
options.description: When enabled the PP cost of Psynergy is randomized within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: randomize_psynergy_aoe
options.description: When enabled the AoE of Psynergy is randomized

options.name: adjust_enemy_psynergy_power
options.description: When enabled the power of Enemy Psynergy is randomized within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: randomize_enemy_psynergy_aoe
options.description: When enabled the AoE of Enemy Psynergy is randomized.

options.name: enemy_elemental_resistance
options.description:
Determine how Enemy Elemental Resistance is shuffled
Vanilla: leaves the elemental resistance as per the vanilla game.
Shuffle Elemental Resistance: will shuffle them between enemies.
Randomize Elemental Resistance: will randomize the resistances for each enemy.

options.name: shuffle_djinn
options.description:
How Djinn should be placed in your own world. The client has extra commands to help find which Djinni locations have (not) been checked.
Note currently Djinn can only be placed in djinn locations in their own world due to game limitations.
Vanilla keeps them in their vanilla locations.
Vanilla Shuffled by Element, Djinni are placed in vanilla Djinni locations that share their element.
Vanilla Shuffled are placed in any vanilla Djinni location regardless of element.

options.name: shuffle_djinn_stat_boosts
options.description: When enabled the stats a djinn grant are shuffled amongst each other.

options.name: adjust_djinn_attack_power
options.description: When enabled the attack power of djinn are randomized within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: randomize_djinn_attack_aoe
options.description: When enabled the Area of Effect of djinn used in battle is randomized.

options.name: scale_djinni_battle_difficulty
options.description: Adjust Djinn battle difficulty based on number of owned Djinn.

options.name: randomize_summon_costs
options.description: When enabled the costs for Summons is randomized.

options.name: adjust_summon_power
options.description: when enabled the power of Summons is randomized within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: randomize_equip_compatibility
options.description:
When enabled the compatability for each equipment piece is randomized. 
Compatibility defines what each character can equip.

options.name: adjust_equip_prices
options.description: When enabled the price for each equipment piece is randomized within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: adjust_equip_stats
options.description: When enabled the stats for each equipment piece is randomized within a margin of vanilla.

options.name: shuffle_weapon_attack
options.description: When enabled, the attack stat from weapons is shuffled amongst each other

options.name: shuffle_weapon_effect
options.description: When enabled the effects for weapons are shuffled amongst each other.

options.name: shuffle_armour_defense
options.description: When enabled, the defense from equipment is shuffled amongst each other

options.name: shuffle_armour_effect
options.description: When enabled the bonus effects for armour are shuffled amongst each other.

options.name: randomize_curses
options.description: When enabled the curses for equipment are randomized.

options.name: remove_all_curses
options.description: When enabled, curses are removed.

options.name: start_with_healing_psynergy
options.description: Start the game with at least one healing Psynergy (Cure, Ply, Wish or Aura)

options.name: start_with_revive
options.description: When enabled, start the game with Revive Psynergy

options.name: show_items_outside_chest
options.description:
Chests and Tablets are replaced with their contents on the floor.
This allows for scouting items. Note certain locations are still not visible,
for example items in pots or barrels.

options.name: free_avoid
options.description: When enabled, the Avoid Psynergy will cost no PP

options.name: free_retreat
options.description: When enabled, the Retreat Psynergy will cost no PP

options.name: scale_exp
options.description: Scale how much Exp is earned by the party.

options.name: scale_coins
options.description: Scale how much Coins are earned by the party.

options.name: starting_levels
options.description:
Determine the starting levels for characters joining the party.
Note this only increases levels of characters that are lower level than the party

options.name: sanctum_revive_cost
options.description:
Determine how expensive the Sanctuary is to revive characters
Vanilla: leaves the cost per the vanilla game (20x level)
Reduced: will alter the cost to be cheaper than vanilla (2x level)
Fixed: will alter the cost to the same price throughout (100 coins)

options.name: avoid_always_works
options.description: When enabled, Avoid always succeeds and will disable encounters. Using it again will enable encounters

options.name: enable_hard_mode
options.description: When enabled, all enemies will have 50% more health, 25% more attack and defense

options.name: reduced_encounter_rate
options.description: When enabled, the encounter rate will be halved

options.name: easier_bosses
options.description: When enabled, boss fights will be easier by altering their scripts / stats

options.name: manual_retreat_glitch
options.description:
When enabled, Hold select to trigger the Retreat glitch. Logic does not require or expect use of glitches to beat your seed,
this purely gives more control to activating the glitch over draining your PP to than use it anyway.

options.name: shuffle_music
options.description: When enabled, music is shuffled amongst each other.

options.name: teleport_to_dungeons_and_towns
options.description: When enabled, allows Teleport to target small villages and dungeons

options.name: auto_run
options.description: Swaps the behavior of holding B when moving (default is now run instead of walk)

options.name: disable_shop_gametickets
options.description:
Disable Shopkeepers offering Game Tickets, in Vanilla this is the main way to get game tickets. 
In the randomizer they and/or their rewards may show up as part of the item pool (see common_consumable_filler_weight and lucky_equipment_filler_weight options).
Note disabling this setting will make shop interactions take longer each time a game ticket is offered.

options.name: trap_chance
options.description:
The chance for filler items in the pool to be replaced by a trap. This can vary from about 70 to 130 traps depending on your settings. Have fun!
Note that this does not replace every filler item as some options force add filler items to the pool.

options.name: mimic_trap_weight
options.description:
The weight for a trap to be a Mimic. As not all vanilla locations can be mimics.
Mimics will drop their vanilla contents which tend to be more useful consumables but can also be things like Game Tickets or Lucky Medals.
Note: Enabling this will force a number of filler items to the pool to ensure generation success due to locations not supporting mimics.

options.name: scale_mimics
options.description: Whether the strength of mimics should be scaled based on the sphere they are in.  Increases generation time

options.name: forge_material_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be a forge material. These will be forged into any of their regular results.
Note that forging results are RNG based in the game and the randomizer does not alter this behaviour.
Examples of materials are Tear Stone, Sylph Feather, Golem Core, Dark Matter and Orihalcon.

options.name: forge_materials_are_filler
options.description: Whether Forge Materials should be marked as filler instead of useful

options.name: rusty_material_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be a rusty weapon. These weapons will forge into their counterparts as per usual.
E.g. you can find a Rusty Sword which when forged turns into the Soul Brand or a Rusty Staff for a Glower Staff.

options.name: stat_boost_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be a stat boost item.
These are Cookie, Hard Nut, Apple, Mint, Power Bread and Lucky Pepper.

options.name: uncommon_consumable_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be an uncommon consumable.
Examples of these are Psy Crystal, Mist Potion, Potion and Water of Life.

options.name: forged_equipment_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be the end result of forging equipment through materials or rusty weapons.
Examples are Excalibur, Viking Axe, Goblin's Rod, Luna Shield, Astral Circlet and Dragon Boots.

options.name: lucky_equipment_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be a lucky item reward from the fountain in Lemuria or Lucky Wheels in Contigo.
Examples of these are Hestia Blade, Mighty Axe, Aegis Shield, Crown of Glory, Quick Boots, Silk Shirt and War Ring.

options.name: artifacts_are_filler
options.description:
Whether "Rare" Equipment should be considered filler instead of useful.  Rings, Shirts, and Boots are
not reclassified by this option.

options.name: shop_equipment_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be equipment from the shop.
These include the normal things you can buy amongst a few of the artefacts. Think along the lines of Long Sword, Silver Helm, Chain Mail to a Frost Wand.

options.name: coins_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be coins.
The coin amounts are the vanilla coin items you can find. These vary from 3 coins to 911 coins. Majority is around or below 300.

options.name: common_consumable_filler_weight
options.description:
The weight for a filler item to be a common consumable.
Examples of these are Herbs, Vials, Antidotes, Elixirs, Smoke Bombs, Lucky Medals and Game Tickets

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
